note: Loose roles common THROUGHOUT the world — the familiar shapes every culture produces (mediator, warrior, warden, healer, keeper, maker, seeker, trader). The role is universal; the METHOD comes entirely from training + locale + disposition. Same role, opposite expression across the axes: a Stillhold mediator buries the truth to keep peace; a Marchward mediator speaks it plainly. This is the connective tissue that makes the world legible (you always know roughly what a 'warrior' or 'healer' does) AND infinitely varied (HOW they do it is local). Weaves through NPCs, origins, generation (SNG-020), and the Reaches. Authored by Aevi (PO), from Erik's universal-roles direction.
principle: A role is a NEED every human community has — someone to settle disputes, someone to fight, someone to tend the hurt, someone to keep the knowledge. The need is universal; the answer is local. The same role, dropped into different dispositions, becomes wildly different practice — and that difference IS the world's texture. When you meet a 'mediator' you know their function; you do NOT know their method until you know where they trained and what disposition steeps them. The diversity and the extremes drive the narrative: a life-warden finds meaning tending the neglected bodies of the mind-obsessed Cogitants precisely because the role travels and the need appears where a locale can't meet it itself.

roles.universalNeed: someone to settle disputes and hold the peace between parties

expressions.how: mediates by BURYING the truth — keeps peace by ensuring the unsayable stays unsaid; a master of the gentle deflection
mediates by SPEAKING plainly — lays the real grievance on the table because pretense gets people killed at the border
mediates by FEELING — reads the room's hurt and moves it, resolves by warmth and motion rather than terms
mediates by STRUCTURE — binds a dispute into a contract so precise it can't be broken; peace as enforceable law
Mediator's Tongue — the folk-craft, a bit of all of these, none to mastery

roles.trainingCraft: Stillcraft at the peace-pole; Latticework for the binding kind; folk Mediator's Tongue in the Valley

roles.universalNeed: someone to fight, defend, and end threats
roles.methodVaries: as widely as the axes — styles and abilities utterly unalike

expressions.how: the Edge — disciplined, formal, economical force; fights by the forms, ends fast, never brawls
the raider's way — feral, improvised, meaner-than-the-badland survival fighting
the war-thinker — never lifts a blade; wins by out-modeling the enemy, moving armies and traps by pure Noesis
the guardian — martial mastery held as devotion and defense, never aggression; the Soma-warrior who protects and will not start
the unseen blade — wins by never being where the enemy strikes; Umbracraft turns war into a thing that happens in the dark

roles.trainingCraft: the Edge, Soma, Noesis, Umbracraft, Wildcraft — a 'warrior' can be built from almost any tradition; the axis chooses the style

roles.universalNeed: someone to tend, heal, and keep the vulnerable
roles.methodVaries: by which pole they steep in — and the role famously TRAVELS to where a locale can't tend itself

expressions.how: the Vivimancer-warden — tends by quickening, healing, growing; life poured into the failing
the Ashwarden — tends ENDINGS; the mercy of a clean death, grief-work, the body eased out

THE LIFE-WARDEN WHO FOUND THEIR WORK — a Vivimancer or body-tender who travels TO the Cogitarium because the mind-obsessed neglect their own failing bodies; finds meaning tending flesh a whole culture has abandoned. The role appearing exactly where the locale can't meet its own need. (Erik's example, canon.)
the Green Warden — tends the Accord and the grove itself, not just bodies; Isolde's road

roles.trainingCraft: Vivimancy, Palework, Greenlore, Numenwork — and a willingness to carry the craft to where it's needed

roles.universalNeed: someone to hold the knowledge, the records, the memory
roles.methodVaries: by what their locale values keeping and how

expressions.how: the Guardian — keeps by controlling access; knowledge as a thing to be protected and rationed
the memory-palace keeper — holds vast knowledge IN the mind by Noesis, no archive needed, the librarian who IS the library
the road-keeper — keeps by STORY and song, memory carried in the telling, the Storykeeper's living craft
the Ent — keeps by BEING old; memory as lived duration, a grudge or an accord held across centuries because they were simply there
the Keeper of the Unsaid — keeps a truth by ensuring no one speaks it; knowledge held by suppression

roles.trainingCraft: Storykeeper, Noesis, Latticework, and the folk archival crafts

roles.universalNeed: someone to build, mend, and make
roles.methodVaries: by the substrate and grammar of their locale

expressions.how: the Enginewright — builds machine-systems, Precursor works, at the deepest technical level
the Churnfolk maker — improvises wonders that work once; invention as luck-riding (Orrin's way)
the block-crafter — builds by the manifest grammar, structures raised in minutes by recipe-logic
Tinker's Hand — the folk-making craft, practical and unglamorous

roles.trainingCraft: Enginecraft, Wildcraft, Tinker's Hand, and any manifest-domain grammar

roles.universalNeed: someone who finds the hidden truth — of a place, a person, a mystery

expressions.how: reveals by Radiance — drags the hidden into the burning light, strips all concealment
reads by the Seeing Dark — finds what light would destroy, sees into hidden hearts
the rune-caster — reads the pole-disposition of a thing by the throw; divination as coordinate-reading
True Attention — attends so fully the truth surfaces; the Watcher's way (Aevi's own)

roles.trainingCraft: Radiance, Umbracraft, Numenwork, rune-casting, folk Prism Sight / Keen Appraisal

weaveGuidance.forNpcs: Every NPC is (at least loosely) one of these roles + a local method. When authoring or generating an NPC, pick the ROLE (the need they fill) then let their locale/disposition set the METHOD. This is why the same role reads utterly different across the world and why the player can still orient ('ah, a warrior — but a Cogitarium war-thinker, not a Redline duelist').
weaveGuidance.forOrigins: Character origins can be framed as role + homeland: a Redline Warrior, a Hollow-March Warden, a Wend Keeper. The role gives the player a familiar handle; the homeland gives them the method and craft-access (ties SNG-017 creation).
weaveGuidance.forGeneration: SNG-020 generation picks a role for a needed NPC, then derives method from the current area's disposition — a generated 'healer' in the Cogitarium is a body-tender working against the local grain; a generated 'mediator' in the Stillhold buries truth. Role + disposition = a consistent, varied, never-generic person.
weaveGuidance.forTravel: Roles TRAVEL. A warden goes where bodies are neglected; a mediator where disputes fester; a maker where something must be built. An NPC far from home is often a role carrying its craft to a need the locale can't meet — instant story (the life-warden in the Cogitarium). The GM should place traveled-roles as living evidence the world's needs cross its regions.
weaveGuidance.theEngine: Diversity and extremes drive narrative: put two expressions of the same role in a room (a Redline warrior and a Cogitarium war-thinker; a life-warden and an Ashwarden) and they generate story by contrast alone — same need, opposite method, mutual incomprehension or hard-won respect. Populate for this friction.

roleFunctionLink.note: Roles are defined by the FUNCTIONS they must cover; the domain sets HOW. A role's practitioner (authored or generated) needs a kit spanning their role's functions. This is the bridge between universal_roles and skill_functions.
roleFunctionLink.generationTie: When generating a role-instance, ensure their abilities cover their role's functions via the local domain's methods — a Cogitarium Warden covers heal/shield by Noesis-adjacent body-tending against the grain; a Redline Warrior covers strike/shield by the Edge.
